name: KrillApp.Executor.Compute
title: Compute
description: Performs statistical analysis on historical data from an input DataPoint over a configured time range. Fetches the data series for the selected period (hour, day, week, month, or year) and applies a statistical operation (average, median, high, low, sum, count, range, first, last, or standard deviation). The computed result is stored in the Compute node's own meta.snapshot; downstream observers pull it from there.
shortDescription: Computes a statistical summary of a DataPoint's historical values over a time range and stores the result in its own snapshot.
llmPurpose: Computes a statistical summary (average, min, max, sum, count, etc.) of an input DataPoint's historical data over a time range and stores the result in its own meta.snapshot for observers to pull.
llmBehavior: invoked when an observed source completes (SOURCE_INVOKED), e.g. a CronTimer
fetches historical data series from the DataPoint listed in meta.inputs
applies statistical operation over the time range
stores the result in its own meta.snapshot; nodes observing this node are invoked
llmOutputs: computed statistic stored in own meta.snapshot; observers pull it from there
llmPromptHints: what was the max this week
llmCreationHints: Create a Compute node when the user needs statistical analysis of historical data. List the DataPoint to analyze in meta.inputs, choose a time range and statistical operation, and list the invoking schedule/trigger in meta.sources with SOURCE_INVOKED. To persist the result, wire a DataPoint to observe this Compute node (its own sources and inputs).

llmConnectionHints.role: analysis node that reads an input DataPoint's history and stores summary statistics in its own snapshot
llmConnectionHints.wiring: Observer-side: list the schedule/trigger in sources with SOURCE_INVOKED; list the DataPoint whose history to analyze in inputs; a DataPoint wanting the statistic observes this node via its own sources+inputs.

llmExamples: Compute the daily average of this temperature sensor.
What was the maximum pressure reading this week?
Summarize the last month of data for this sensor.
Count how many readings were recorded today.
